# Regression test for 'f' (direction) and 'd' (delete) on aisle-edge
# selections. The UI exposes two selection scopes:
#
#   * chain — single click on an aisle edge selects the entire
#     collinear run (the whole row or column)
#   * segment — double-click narrows the selection to just the clicked
#     sub-edge
#
# For both 'f' and 'd', the emitted annotation should cover the full
# chain in chain mode and just the clicked sub-edge in segment mode.
# Chain-mode annotations additionally carry an `anchor` that pins the
# marker to the clicked sub-edge (UI-only; engine ignores it).
#
# Note: each action regenerates the aisle graph, so later edge-selects
# at the same world point may pick different seed indices. What we
# care about is the annotation scope (extents + anchor), not the seed
# numbering.

polygon outer
0,0
400,0
400,300
0,300
----
polygon outer: 4 vertices

set stalls_per_face=4
----
set stalls_per_face=4

debug use_abstract_stamp=true
----
debug use_abstract_stamp=true

generate
----
total_stalls: 162

# --- Chain-mode direction on a horizontal row. ---
# The click lands on a cross-aisle sub-edge; chain expansion walks the
# whole row, so the annotation extents span every cell in the row.
edge-select near=200,120 mode=chain
----
edge-select seed=164 mode=chain chain_len=8

edge-cycle
----
edge-cycle

annotations
----
direction on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=y coord=2 range=lattice:-8,lattice:1 traffic=two-way-oriented

# --- Segment-mode direction on the same row. ---
# With mode=segment, the extents collapse to just the seed sub-edge
# (no anchor — extents == seed). 'f' on this distinct scope creates a
# second annotation rather than cycling the row-wide one.
edge-select near=200,120 mode=segment
----
edge-select seed=164 mode=segment chain_len=1

edge-cycle
----
edge-cycle

annotations
----
direction on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=y coord=2 range=lattice:-8,lattice:1 traffic=two-way-oriented
direction on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=y coord=2 range=lattice:-4,lattice:-3 traffic=two-way-oriented

# --- Chain-mode delete on a vertical column. ---
# The click at (200,180) lands on a column aisle; chain expansion
# picks up the entire column (constant x, spanning several y cells).
edge-select near=200,180 mode=chain
----
edge-select seed=98 mode=chain chain_len=6

edge-delete
----
edge-delete

annotations
----
direction on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=y coord=2 range=lattice:-8,lattice:1 traffic=two-way-oriented
direction on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=y coord=2 range=lattice:-4,lattice:-3 traffic=two-way-oriented
delete-edge on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=x coord=-4 range=lattice:0,lattice:6

# --- Segment-mode delete on a horizontal sub-edge. ---
# After the chain delete above the graph regenerates; the closest
# remaining edge at (200,180) is a single cross-aisle sub-edge. In
# segment mode the annotation scope matches that sub-edge exactly,
# and no anchor is emitted (extents == seed).
edge-select near=200,180 mode=segment
----
edge-select seed=184 mode=segment chain_len=1

edge-delete
----
edge-delete

annotations
----
direction on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=y coord=2 range=lattice:-8,lattice:1 traffic=two-way-oriented
direction on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=y coord=2 range=lattice:-4,lattice:-3 traffic=two-way-oriented
delete-edge on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=x coord=-4 range=lattice:0,lattice:6
delete-edge on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=y coord=4 range=lattice:-5,lattice:-3
